name: HŠK Zrinjski Mostar
content:
HŠK Zrinjski Mostar is a professional football club based in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and is widely regarded as the oldest football club in the country, having been founded in 1905. The club takes its name from the historic Croatian noble family Zrinski and traditionally represents the Croat community in the region. Zrinjski plays its home matches at the Stadion pod Bijelim Brijegom and is commonly known by the nickname “Plemići” (The Noblemen).

The club has a long and complex history shaped by political events. It was active in the early 20th century but was banned in 1945 by the Yugoslav authorities following World War II, along with several other clubs. This ban lasted until 1992, when Zrinjski was re-established after the independence of Bosnia and Herzegovina. During the 1990s, the club competed in regional competitions before joining the unified Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina in 2000.

Since the 2000s, Zrinjski has become one of the most successful clubs in Bosnian football. The team won its first national championship in the 2004–05 season and has since secured multiple league titles and domestic cups, establishing itself as a dominant force in the country. In addition to domestic success, Zrinjski regularly participates in European competitions and made history by becoming the first club from Bosnia and Herzegovina to reach the group stage of a UEFA competition in the 2023–24 season.

name: FK Željezničar Sarajevo
content:
FK Željezničar Sarajevo is a professional football club based in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1921 by railway workers, which is reflected in its name (“Željezničar” meaning “railway worker”). The club is commonly known as “Željo” and plays its home matches at the Grbavica Stadium. Throughout its history, it has built a strong identity as a working-class club and is widely recognized for developing talented players through its youth academy.

During the Yugoslav era, Željezničar achieved its greatest domestic success by winning the Yugoslav First League in the 1971–72 season. The club also made a significant mark in European competitions, most notably reaching the semi-finals of the UEFA Cup in the 1984–85 season, which remains one of the best international achievements by any club from Bosnia and Herzegovina.

After the breakup of Yugoslavia and the Bosnian War, the club faced serious difficulties, including damage to its stadium and interruptions in competition. However, it recovered and became one of the most successful teams in independent Bosnia and Herzegovina, winning multiple national league titles and domestic cups. Today, Željezničar remains one of the country’s most popular and historically important football clubs.

name: FK Sarajevo
content:
FK Sarajevo is a professional football club based in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1946 following the merger of local teams Udarnik and Sloboda. Initially established under different names such as Torpedo and SDM Sarajevo, the club adopted its current name in 1949. It quickly became one of the most prominent teams in Yugoslav football, regularly competing in the Yugoslav First League and establishing a strong presence at the national level.

The club achieved its greatest success during the Yugoslav era by winning the Yugoslav First League in the 1966–67 season, becoming the first club from Bosnia and Herzegovina to claim the title. This victory was a historic breakthrough, ending years of dominance by clubs from Serbia and Croatia. FK Sarajevo also participated in early European competitions during the 1960s, marking its emergence on the international stage.

After the independence of Bosnia and Herzegovina, FK Sarajevo established itself as one of the country’s most successful clubs. It has won multiple titles in the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina and several domestic cups, remaining a consistent contender in national competitions. The club has also competed regularly in UEFA tournaments, representing Bosnia and Herzegovina in European qualifiers and maintaining its status as one of the leading football institutions in the country.

name: FK Borac Banja Luka
content:
FK Borac Banja Luka is a professional football club based in Banja Luka, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 4 July 1926. Originally established as a workers’ club under the name RSK Borac, its identity has long been tied to labor movements, with the name “Borac” meaning “fighter.” The club plays its home matches at the Banja Luka City Stadium and is widely regarded as one of the most popular and successful teams in Republika Srpska.

Throughout its history, Borac competed in the Yugoslav league system, gradually rising through the divisions and becoming a recognized team at the national level. After World War II, it established itself as the leading club in Banja Luka and continued to develop within Yugoslav football. One of its greatest achievements came with winning the Yugoslav Cup, and the club also gained international recognition by winning the Mitropa Cup, highlighting its success beyond domestic competitions.

In the modern era, Borac has been one of the leading clubs in Bosnia and Herzegovina. It has won the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the national cup, regularly competing in European tournaments such as the UEFA Champions League and Europa competitions. Today, the club remains a key force in Bosnian football, representing Banja Luka at both domestic and international levels.

name: NK Čelik Zenica
content:
NK Čelik Zenica is a professional football club based in Zenica, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 16 June 1945 by a group of World War II veterans. The club’s name, “Čelik” (meaning “steel”), reflects the city’s strong industrial and metallurgical identity. Known as the “Black-Reds,” Čelik plays its home matches at Bilino Polje Stadium, which is also used by the Bosnia and Herzegovina national team.

During the Yugoslav era, Čelik established itself as a competitive club, spending 17 seasons in the Yugoslav First League. Its greatest international success came in the 1970s, when it won the Mitropa Cup twice and also achieved success in the Intertoto Cup. After the independence of Bosnia and Herzegovina, Čelik became one of the dominant teams in the country, winning three consecutive national league titles from 1994 to 1997 and two domestic cups in 1995 and 1996.

In recent years, the club has faced financial and organizational challenges, leading to relegation from the top tier and a temporary drop to lower divisions. Despite these difficulties, Čelik remains one of the most historic and supported clubs in Bosnia and Herzegovina, notable for its strong fan base and its unique model as a fan-owned club, where members democratically elect the leadership.

name: NK Široki Brijeg
content:
NK Široki Brijeg is a professional football club based in Široki Brijeg, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1948. The team plays its home matches at the Pecara Stadium and is commonly known by the nickname “Plavi” (The Blues). It currently competes in the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina, the top tier of football in the country.

The club’s history includes several name changes and participation in different league systems. During the Yugoslav period, it competed under the name NK Mladost Lištica, before adopting its current name in 1995. In the 1990s, Široki Brijeg became the most successful club in the First League of Herzeg-Bosnia, winning the title five times. Following the unification of Bosnian football in 2000, the club joined the national top division and established itself as a consistent competitor.

Since the early 2000s, Široki Brijeg has been one of the notable clubs in Bosnia and Herzegovina, winning the Premier League twice and the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup multiple times. The team has also regularly appeared in UEFA competitions, including qualifiers for the UEFA Champions League and Europa League, although it has not yet reached the group stage. Today, it remains an important and competitive club in Bosnian football.

name: HNK Brotnjo
content:
HNK Brotnjo is a football club based in Čitluk, Bosnia and Herzegovina. The club was founded in 1955 and represents the Brotnjo region in Herzegovina, from which it takes its name. It plays its home matches at the Bare Stadium in Čitluk and competes in the lower tiers of the Bosnian football league system. The club is commonly known as a community-focused team with strong local support.

Historically, Brotnjo’s most successful period came around the late 1990s and early 2000s. The club won the First League of Herzeg-Bosnia and later achieved success in the unified Bosnian football system, including winning the 1999–2000 season play-off, which qualified them for top-tier competition. This success also allowed the club to participate in European competitions such as the UEFA Champions League qualifiers, marking the highest point in its history.

In later years, HNK Brotnjo moved between different levels of the Bosnian league pyramid, mostly competing in the second and lower divisions. Despite not maintaining top-flight status, the club remains active and is known for its youth development and regional tournaments. It continues to be an important sports institution in Čitluk and Herzegovina, maintaining its identity as a traditional local football club.

name: FK Modriča
content:
FK Modriča is a professional football club based in Modriča, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1922. The club’s early history goes back to the interwar period, when several predecessor teams such as Zora, Olimpija, and Dobor existed before World War II. After the war, the modern club structure was established in 1945 under the name Sloga, later changing through several names before becoming FK Modriča. The team plays its home matches at the Dr. Milan Jelić Stadium.

During the Yugoslav era, FK Modriča competed mostly in lower regional and amateur competitions, with one of its notable early achievements being the amateur championship of SR Bosnia and Herzegovina in the 1968–69 season. The club rose in prominence in the 2000s, winning the First League of Republika Srpska in 2003 and the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup in 2004. These successes helped them reach the top national division.

The club’s greatest achievement came in the 2007–08 season, when FK Modriča won the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina, securing its first and only national league title. This made them one of the few clubs outside the traditional powerhouses to win the championship. They also competed in European competitions, including UEFA Champions League qualifiers, marking the peak period in the club’s history.

name: FK Leotar
content:
FK Leotar is a professional football club based in Trebinje, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 19 August 1925. The club is named after the nearby Leotar mountain and plays its home matches at the Stadion Police, which has a capacity of around 8,500 spectators. Leotar is commonly known as the “Tigers” and currently competes in the First League of the Republika Srpska, the second tier of Bosnian football.

During the Yugoslav period, FK Leotar never reached the top national division, but it remained a stable regional club with a long tradition. After the Bosnian War, it became part of the football system in Republika Srpska and later joined the unified Bosnian league structure in the early 2000s. The club’s breakthrough came in the 2001–02 season when it won the First League of Republika Srpska, earning promotion to the national top flight.

The greatest achievement in Leotar’s history came in the 2002–03 season, when it surprisingly won the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina, becoming national champions for the first time. This title also qualified the club for the UEFA Champions League qualifiers, marking its only appearance in Europe’s premier club competition. Although it was eliminated in the early qualifying rounds, this period remains the peak of Leotar’s success.

name: FK Velež Mostar
content:
FK Velež Mostar is a professional football club based in Mostar, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 26 June 1922. The club is named after the nearby Mount Velež, and is commonly known by its nickname “Rođeni” (The Born). It traditionally represents a multi-ethnic identity and has been one of the most popular clubs in Herzegovina throughout its history. Velež plays its home matches at the Stadion Rođeni, although during its most successful period it used the historic Bijeli Brijeg Stadium.

During the Yugoslav era, Velež was considered one of the strongest clubs from Bosnia and Herzegovina, regularly competing in the Yugoslav First League and often finishing in the upper half of the table. The club achieved major success in cup competitions, winning the Yugoslav Cup twice (1981 and 1986) and reaching the UEFA Cup quarter-finals in 1974–75, which remains one of its greatest European achievements.

After the breakup of Yugoslavia and the Bosnian War, Velež lost access to its historic stadium and went through a difficult rebuilding period. The club eventually returned to top-flight football in Bosnia and Herzegovina and regained stability. In modern times, it has re-established itself as a competitive Premier League club and also won the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup in 2021–22, marking a strong revival of its sporting success.

name: FK Slavija Sarajevo
content:
FK Slavija Sarajevo is a football club based in Istočno Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina. It was originally founded in 1908, making it one of the oldest football clubs in the country. The club was formed by Sarajevo gymnasium students under the name Đački sportski klub (SSK), and it developed during the Austro-Hungarian period, when early football activities in the city were still informal and heavily restricted. Over time, it became known as Slavija and adopted red and white colors inspired by Slavia Prague.

In the interwar period, Slavija was one of the strongest clubs in Bosnia and Herzegovina. It competed in the national championships of the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (later the Kingdom of Yugoslavia), where it played multiple top-flight seasons and even reached the runner-up position in the 1935–36 season. During this era, it was considered the most successful Bosnian club in the Yugoslav league system.

After World War II and the breakup of Yugoslavia, the club was re-established in the 1990s and later joined the football system of Republika Srpska. It achieved modern success by winning the First League of Republika Srpska (2003–04) and later reached the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina, where it even finished as runner-up in the 2008–09 season. Slavija also won the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup (2008–09), marking its most successful modern period.

name: FK Sloboda Tuzla
content:
FK Sloboda Tuzla is a professional football club based in Tuzla, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 30 November 1919, making it one of the oldest football clubs in the country. The club is commonly known as “Crveno-crni” (The Red-Blacks) and plays its home matches at Stadion Tušanj. Sloboda is part of the larger multi-sport society RSD Sloboda Tuzla, which includes several sports sections across different disciplines.

Throughout its history, Sloboda has experienced both Yugoslav and Bosnian football systems. In the Yugoslav period, the club competed mainly in the Second League and occasionally reached higher levels, while also achieving success in domestic cup competitions. After the breakup of Yugoslavia, Sloboda became a regular participant in the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina, though it has also spent periods in the First League of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina due to relegation and restructuring.

In modern times, FK Sloboda Tuzla has remained an important traditional club with a strong local identity and supporter base known as “Fukare Tuzla.” While the club has struggled with consistency and financial challenges, it has also had notable achievements, including reaching cup finals and producing several well-known players. Sloboda continues to be one of the historically significant clubs in Bosnian football and a key representative of the city of Tuzla.

name: FK Tuzla City
content:
FK Tuzla City is a professional football club based in Tuzla, Bosnia and Herzegovina, originally founded in 1955 under the name FK Sloga Simin Han. The club is based in the Simin Han suburb of Tuzla and plays its home matches at the Tušanj City Stadium. It is commonly known by nicknames such as “Građani” (The Citizens) and “Plavi” (The Blues).

For most of its history, the club competed in lower divisions of Bosnian football. A major breakthrough came in the 2017–18 season, when it won the First League of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and earned promotion to the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina for the first time. Shortly after this success, in 2018, the club officially changed its name from FK Sloga Simin Han to FK Tuzla City.

Since reaching the top tier, Tuzla City has established itself as a competitive Premier League side, with one of its best performances coming in the 2019–20 season, when it finished 5th in the league and came close to qualifying for European competitions. The club has also participated in UEFA qualifiers, marking its first appearances on the continental stage, and continues to represent Tuzla alongside the city’s traditional club FK Sloboda Tuzla.

name: FK Radnički Lukavac
content:
FK Radnički Lukavac is a professional football club based in Lukavac, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1921. The club represents the industrial identity of the city and is traditionally associated with workers’ sports culture, as reflected in its name “Radnički” (meaning “Workers”). It plays its home matches at Stadion Jošik, which has a capacity of around 3,000 spectators.

The club has spent most of its history moving between lower and regional levels of Yugoslav and Bosnian football, but it experienced its strongest period in the mid-1990s. During the 1995–96 season, Radnički finished runner-up in the top domestic league, and in 1996–97 it reached the semi-finals of the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup, marking its peak competitive performance.

In the modern era, FK Radnički Lukavac has mostly competed in the lower tiers of the Bosnian football pyramid, including the First and Second Leagues of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The club remains an important local institution with a strong supporter base known as the Sioux, and it maintains a traditional rivalry with Sloboda Tuzla, which is one of the most notable regional matchups in Tuzla Canton football.

name: NK Bosna Visoko
content:
NK Bosna Visoko is a professional football club based in Visoko, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1953 through the merger of two local clubs, NK Jadran (1923) and NK Radnički (1934). The club plays its home matches at Stadion Luke and is commonly known by the nickname “Faraoni” (The Pharaohs). It represents one of the most traditional sports institutions in the city of Visoko.

During the Yugoslav period, NK Bosna achieved several notable promotions, including reaching the Yugoslav Second League in 1963, which was one of its strongest early accomplishments. The club later returned to the second tier in 1978, but was relegated again in the early 1980s. After the Bosnian War, Bosna experienced a successful period in the late 1990s, including winning the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup in 1998–99 and the Supercup in 1999, marking the peak of its domestic success.

In the modern era, NK Bosna Visoko has mostly competed in the First and Second Leagues of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, moving between divisions due to financial and sporting fluctuations. The club also came close to returning to the top tier in the 2016–17 season but narrowly missed promotion. Despite this, Bosna remains an important club in Bosnian football with a strong local identity and a history of producing competitive squads at regional level.

name: FK Rudar Kakanj
content:
FK Rudar Kakanj is a professional football club based in Kakanj, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 3 May 1920, making it one of the older clubs in the country. The club is traditionally linked to the mining industry, which is reflected in its name “Rudar” (meaning “miner”). It plays its home matches at Stadion Rudara, which has a capacity of around 5,000 spectators.

Throughout its history, Rudar Kakanj has mostly competed between the second and lower tiers of Bosnian football, with occasional strong periods in the First League of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The club has finished as runner-up in that competition several times (notably in the 2005–06, 2006–07, and 2008–09 seasons), showing that it has often been close to top-tier promotion.

In modern football, FK Rudar Kakanj continues to compete mainly in the Second League of the FBiH, after recent relegations and promotions between divisions. Despite not being a top-flight team, it remains an important local club with a strong identity, known for developing players and having a long-standing tradition in Bosnian football history.

name: FK Budućnost Banovići
content:
FK Budućnost Banovići is a professional football club based in Banovići, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1947. The club’s name “Budućnost” means “Future,” and it is strongly connected to the local mining community, as Banovići is a well-known coal mining town. The team plays its home matches at Stadion FK Budućnost, which is one of the key sports venues in the region.

Throughout its history, Budućnost Banovići has mostly competed in the second and third tiers of Yugoslav and Bosnian football. In the post-war period, the club became a regular participant in the First League of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, often competing near the top of the table and occasionally challenging for promotion to the Premier League.

In modern times, FK Budućnost Banovići has remained a stable second-tier club, known for its strong local support and player development. While it has not yet established itself in the top division, it continues to be an important part of Bosnian football, representing the mining region of Banovići and maintaining a consistent presence in national competitions.

name: HNK Orašje
content:
HNK Orašje is a football club based in Orašje, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1996. The club is known as “Labudovi” (The Swans) and plays its home matches at the Gradski Stadion Orašje, which has a capacity of around 3,000–3,500 spectators. It represents the Croatian community of the town and competes in the Bosnian football league system.

The club’s most successful period came in the mid-2000s. In the 2005–06 season, HNK Orašje won the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup, which is its greatest achievement to date. This success allowed the club to participate in European competition, where it played in the 2006–07 UEFA Cup, but was eliminated in the early qualifying rounds by NK Domžale.

In domestic football, Orašje has mostly competed in the First League of the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina, the second tier of the national system. The club has also had success in regional competitions in the late 1990s and early 2000s, but has generally remained a lower-tier side in modern years, focusing on maintaining stability and local player development.

name: FK Radnik Bijeljina
content:
K Radnik Bijeljina is a professional football club based in Bijeljina, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded on 14 June 1945. The club’s name “Radnik” means “worker,” reflecting its working-class origins. It plays its home matches at the Gradski Stadion Bijeljina, which has a capacity of around 6,000 spectators. Radnik is one of the most established clubs from the northeastern part of the country and competes in the Bosnian football league system.

Historically, the club developed through the Yugoslav league structure, achieving regional success and gradually climbing the system. In the post-war period, Radnik became one of the leading clubs in Republika Srpska football, winning the First League of RS multiple times and earning promotions to the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina. One of its key early milestones was reaching higher-tier Yugoslav competitions after strong regional performances in the 1970s.

Radnik’s greatest modern success came in the 2015–16 season, when it won the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup, its first major national trophy. This victory qualified the club for the UEFA Europa League, marking its debut in European competitions. Since then, Radnik has alternated between the Premier League and lower divisions, but it remains a respected club with a reputation for solid organization and occasional strong cup performances.

name: FK Olimpik
content:
FK Olimpik Sarajevo was a professional football club based in Sarajevo, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in October 1993 during the Bosnian War. The club was established in the Otoka neighbourhood and played its home matches at the Stadion Otoka. It was known by the nickname “Vukovi” (The Wolves) and its colours were mainly green and white.

Olimpik became one of the first clubs formed in independent Bosnia and Herzegovina and quickly joined the national league system. Throughout its existence, it moved between divisions, spending time in both the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina and the First League of the FBiH. The club’s biggest achievement came in the 2014–15 season, when it won the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup, which qualified them for European competition.

In its later years, Olimpik struggled with stability and was relegated from the top tier after the 2020–21 season, finishing last in the Premier League. Shortly after, the club was dissolved in 2021, ending its professional status. Despite its relatively short history, FK Olimpik remains remembered as a notable Sarajevo club that achieved a major cup victory and competed in UEFA competitions.

name: FK Krupa
content:
FK Krupa is a football club based in Krupa na Vrbasu, Bosnia and Herzegovina, founded in 1983. The club is relatively young compared to many traditional Bosnian teams and represents a small settlement near Banja Luka. It plays its home matches at the Gradski Stadion Krupa na Vrbasu, which has a capacity of around 3,500 spectators. The club has often been supported by strong local investment and a stable organizational structure.

FK Krupa’s rise through the football pyramid has been one of its defining features. After competing in lower regional leagues, the club achieved rapid progress in the 2010s. It won promotion to the First League of the Republika Srpska in 2014 and then secured a historic rise to the Premier League of Bosnia and Herzegovina in 2016, marking its debut in the top flight. One of its most notable achievements came in the 2017–18 season, when it reached the Bosnia and Herzegovina Cup final, finishing as runners-up.

In the following seasons, Krupa experienced fluctuations between divisions. The club was relegated from the Premier League in 2019, but quickly returned after winning the First League of RS in the 2019–20 season (later confirmed due to COVID-19 adjustments). It has since continued to move between the top two tiers, establishing itself as a competitive but smaller club in Bosnian football, known for its disciplined structure and occasional strong cup performances.
